insight: Ecuador's performance during hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup has been notable, as they managed to gain momentum in both of their breaks, with no significant change in their pace. This suggests that the team is able to maintain their energy levels and intensity even after the brief pauses in play, which can be a crucial factor in determining the outcome of a match. When Ecuador was pressing before a hydration break, they were able to keep their momentum going afterwards, which is a positive sign as it indicates that they can sustain their attacking pressure. Overall, the statistics imply that hydration breaks have had a neutral to slightly positive effect on Ecuador's gameplay, allowing them to regroup and continue playing at a consistent level.
